title: LLP to Private Limited Company Conversion Online in India
metaDescription: What is LLP to company conversion? Convert LLP to private limited company online with fees, cost, documents required & expert consultant conversion service in India.

hero.headline: LLP to Private Limited Company Conversion in India – Online Process

breadcrumbs.label: LLP to Private Limited Company Conversion

hero.subtext: Access capital, scale faster, and gain credibility with Professionals Law's expert-led LLP to Company conversion.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The conversion of a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) into a Private Limited Company is a strategic move for growing businesses seeking external funding, greater market credibility and expanded operational scope. This process requires the LLP to register as a company with the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) and is governed under the Companies Act, 2013. The key compliance steps include obtaining partners' consent, securing approval from creditors, publication of a public notice and filing necessary e-forms. Professionals Law offers end-to-end support, from document preparation to MCA approval, ensuring a smooth and legally sound transition from LLP to Company structure while meeting all statutory requirements.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: LLPs often face limitations when it comes to raising equity capital, issuing shares or attracting investors. The conversion to a Private Limited Company offers a structured corporate image, easier access to funding and wider scalability. Various regulatory advantages like perpetual succession, share transferability and better tax planning further justify this transformation. Professionals Law facilitates a seamless and compliant conversion process, helping LLPs evolve into dynamic, investor-ready entities with a more recognized corporate identity, while ensuring complete alignment with the latest Companies Act provisions.

body.title: Access to Funding
body.description: After conversion, companies can raise funds through equity shares, venture capital and private placements which are not permitted in an LLP structure.

body.title: Greater Market Credibility
body.description: A Private Limited Company enjoys higher credibility among vendors, financial institutions and investors due to regulated compliance and transparency.

body.title: Limited Liability with Corporate Features
body.description: Shareholders enjoy limited liability while gaining benefits like structured shareholding, profit-sharing and board-governed operations.

body.title: Perpetual Succession
body.description: Unlike LLPs that dissolve on partner exit, companies continue irrespective of ownership changes, ensuring long-term business stability.

body.title: Ease in Expansion
body.description: Companies can expand across states and even internationally with ease, leveraging investor trust and structured governance.

criteria: LLP must have at least 2 designated partners.
LLP must have at least 2 shareholders and 2 directors post-conversion.
LLP must have no secured debts or must have obtained No Objection Certificates (NOCs) from creditors.
LLP must be in active status and compliant with ROC filings.
LLP must have existed for at least one year from the date of incorporation.
All partners must agree to the conversion and become shareholders in the new company.

documents: PAN and Aadhaar of all partners/directors
LLP Agreement and Certificate of Incorporation
Audited Statement of Accounts (not older than 30 days)
Consent of all partners for conversion
Utility bill and rent agreement for the registered office
Digital Signature Certificates (DSCs) of all designated partners
MOA and AOA of the proposed company
Affidavits, declarations and Form URC-1 attachments
List of proposed directors and shareholders

body.description: Pass a resolution approving the conversion from LLP to Company.
File RUN (Reserve Unique Name) for company name approval.
Prepare Memorandum and Articles of Association for the new company.
File URC-1, INC-32 (SPICe+), INC-33 and INC-34 with MCA.
Publish notice in newspapers (English & vernacular) for 21 days as per Section 374(b).
Registrar reviews documents and public objections, if any.
MCA issues new incorporation certificate as a Private Limited Company.
Delay in public notice or non-compliance with Section 366 can lead to rejection or legal notice from the ROC.
False declarations or concealment of facts can attract penalties up to ₹5 lakh and imprisonment under the Companies Act.
Late filing of forms like URC-1 or INC-32 may incur ₹100 per day per form as late fee.

body.title: Document Preparation & Name Approval
body.description: 5 to 7 Working Days

body.title: Public Notice & Newspaper Publication
body.description: 21 Days

body.title: Filing with MCA & Review
body.description: 7 to 10 Working Days

body.title: Issue of COI and PAN
body.description: 3 to 5 Working Days

body.title: Total Estimated Duration
body.description: 30 to 40 Working Days

body.question: Can all LLPs convert into a Private Limited Company?
body.answer: Yes, if the LLP has no secured debts and complies with MCA requirements.

body.question: Is there any minimum capital requirement?
body.answer: No, there is no minimum capital requirement for conversion under the Companies Act.

body.question: Do existing licenses need to be reapplied for?
body.answer: Yes, post-conversion, licenses and registrations need to be updated or reissued.

body.question: Will a new PAN and TAN be required?
body.answer: Yes, a new PAN and TAN will be issued in the name of the new company.

body.question: Are there any tax implications on conversion?
body.answer: If the conversion satisfies all legal conditions, it's generally tax-neutral under the Income Tax Act.

body.question: Is publication of notice mandatory?
body.answer: Yes, notice in two newspapers for 21 days is mandatory under the law.

body.question: Can foreign partners be part of this process?
body.answer: Yes, subject to FDI norms and FEMA compliance.

body.question: Is audit mandatory after conversion?
body.answer: Yes, companies are subject to mandatory statutory audits regardless of turnover.

body.question: Can Professionals Law handle the complete process remotely?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law provides complete online facilitation and documentation support across India.
